task_completion.description: Create a unit square with vertices at (0,0), (1,0), (1,1), and (0,1)
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a square polygon or four connected points at (0,0), (1,0), (1,1), and (0,1) in the GeoGebra graphics view, or corresponding point/polygon definitions in the Algebra view.

task_completion.description: Create the transformed image of the unit square under matrix A
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a parallelogram with vertices at (0,0), (2,1), (3,3), and (1,2) in the GeoGebra graphics view, or the corresponding matrix multiplication/polygon definitions in the Algebra view.

task_completion.description: Include vectors from the origin representing the eigenvectors for each eigenvalue
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for two vectors starting from the origin (0,0). One vector should point along the line y=x (e.g., to (1,1) or (3,3)) and the other along the line y=-x (e.g., to (1,-1)).

task_completion.description: Add a text annotation displaying the eigenvalues and the determinant of the matrix
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a text box on the GeoGebra canvas that explicitly mentions the eigenvalues (3 and 1) and the determinant (3).
Look for the GeoGebra window title displaying 'linear_transform_eigen.ggb' or a save dialog box showing the file being saved to the '~/Documents/GeoGebra/projects/' directory.

integrity.description: Agent used the GeoGebra GUI to create the construction
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the GeoGebra application open with objects being added via the input bar, tools, or algebra view, rather than a terminal window generating the .ggb file via a script.

integrity.description: The construction consists of genuine GeoGebra mathematical objects
integrity.visual_evidence: The Algebra view on the left side of the GeoGebra window should list the points, vectors, polygons, and text objects, proving they are not just an imported static image.

integrity.description: Agent did not bypass the application by writing XML/ZIP directly
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no evidence of the agent opening a text editor or using terminal commands to manually construct the internal XML structure of a .ggb file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Matrix A is [[2,1],[1,2]]. The transformed square must have vertices at (0,0), (2,1), (1,2), and (3,3). The eigenvalues are 3 and 1. The eigenvectors are (1,1) and (1,-1) (or any non-zero scalar multiples). The determinant of A is 3. The expected filename is 'linear_transform_eigen.ggb'.
